prd.oneLiner: Manage feedback intake, deduplication, customer segments, impact assessment, prioritization, roadmap links, and responses in one connected workflow.
prd.goal: Connect 90% of customer feedback to traceable product decisions and increase status responses for major requests.
prd.whyNow: Product feedback is scattered across support tickets, sales calls, surveys, and communities, so priority decisions lose duplicate signals and customer context.
prd.problem: Product feedback is scattered across support tickets, sales calls, surveys, and communities, so priority decisions lose duplicate signals and customer context.
prd.solution: Manage feedback intake, deduplication, customer segments, impact assessment, prioritization, roadmap links, and responses in one connected workflow.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Customer and customer-facing team proposing product improvements
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Product feedback is scattered across support tickets, sales calls, surveys, and communities, so priority decisions lose duplicate signals and customer context.

targets.name: Product operations and customer-insights coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Product feedback is scattered across support tickets, sales calls, surveys, and communities, so priority decisions lose duplicate signals and customer context.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Product feedback is scattered across support tickets, sales calls, surveys, and communities, so priority decisions lose duplicate signals and customer context.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Connect 90% of customer feedback to traceable product decisions and increase status responses for major requests.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Feedback, feature request, problem context, and customer context registration
Feedback, type, customer segment, duplicate, review, and release-status lookup
Duplicate merge, classification, impact assessment, priority review, status communication, and feedback-loop closure
Request volume, segment, priority, release adoption, and feedback-response reporting
Feedback, feature request, problem context, and customer context registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
